hypothesis: Incorporating metal-lattice electron localization function (ELF) descriptors and DFT charge-density convolution features into the ML screening will increase the true positive rate of identifying superhydrides with Tc > 100 K by at least 30% relative to the current lattice-and-composition-based ML workflow when evaluated on a held-out test set of ≥200 candidate metal lattices at 150–300 GPa.
